description: This table tracks all campaign and marketing interactions at the subscriber level. It stores which bonus campaigns and promotional campaigns were sent to each subscriber, when they were sent, how many times, and whether they were delivered successfully or failed. It also tracks whether subscribers responded to campaigns or ignored them (non-responders). It contains campaign response codes, action keys for customer segments, and rolling time window aggregates over 2 days, 7 days, 30 days, 180 days, and 365 days. Use this table when the query involves campaign performance, campaign delivery, campaign response, marketing lifecycle, or any promotional activity tracking. This table does NOT contain revenue, usage, recharge, or subscriber profile information.
granularity: one record per subscriber with rolling aggregates
example_queries: subscribers who did not respond to any campaign in the last 30 days
how many promotions were delivered successfully last week
customers who received bonus campaigns but never responded
never_use_for: revenue, usage, recharge, demographics, device, plan, subscription products

description: This table stores the mapping between subscribers and their assigned audience segments for campaign targeting. Each record links a subscriber MSISDN to a specific segment ID and segment name. It also tracks the execution counter for how many times the segmentation has been run. Use this table when the query is about which campaign audience segment a subscriber belongs to, segment membership, or segment-based audience selection. This is NOT for value-based segmentation or ARPU bands.
granularity: one record per subscriber per segment
example_queries: which audience segment does this subscriber belong to
list all subscribers in segment X
how many times has segmentation been executed
never_use_for: revenue, usage, value segmentation, ARPU, demographics, recharge, campaign delivery status

description: This is the detailed recharge fact table for prepaid subscribers. It contains transaction-level recharge events enriched with subscriber-level rolling aggregates. It stores recharge amount, denomination, channel or type of recharge (e-wallet, retail, bank), recharge date and hour, recharge frequency metrics, rolling recharge revenue aggregates over 1 day, 15 days, 90 days, and 180 days, and average recharge metrics. Use this table when the query specifically asks about recharge behavior, recharge patterns, recharge amounts, recharge channels, top-up frequency, or recharge revenue trends. This table provides the deepest level of recharge analytics with denominations and channels.
granularity: transaction-level events with subscriber-level rolling aggregates
example_queries: average recharge amount in the last 90 days
subscribers who recharge through e-wallet channel
recharge frequency trend over 180 days
most common recharge denomination
never_use_for: voice usage, data usage, SMS, campaign tracking, demographics, plan info, real-time events

description: This is the core table for BOTH base subscription identity and product events. First, it holds master identity attributes: 'Subscription ID', 'Subscription Index', 'MSISDN Index', base line type ('Prepaid' or 'Postpaid'), 'Activation Source', 'Sign-up Method', and 'Subscription Channel'. Second, it tracks individual product subscription events including data bundles, VAS (value-added services), roaming packs, and the total count of active products. Use this table when the query asks for Subscription IDs/Indexes, Prepaid/Postpaid base types, activation sources, OR specific product/bundle/VAS subscriptions.
granularity: one record per subscription identity OR per subscription event
example_queries: numeric Subscription Index or Subscription ID serving as primary key
current subscription type such as Prepaid or Postpaid
Activation Source or Sign-up Method for new customers
subscribers who subscribed to a data bundle via the app
customers with LESS than X active products
never_use_for: total prepaid subscription revenue, overall ARPU, voice/data usage, recharge, demographics (age/gender), plan migrations (old to new plan), credit rating

description: This is the master demographic, device, and plan migration profile table. It stores human attributes (date of birth, gender, nationality), device details (model, technology), external credit rating/score, and tracking for plan migrations (current plan, old plan, new plan, contract movements). Use this table ONLY when the query needs human demographics, plan migration history, credit rating, or device details.
example_queries: subscribers who migrated from prepaid to postpaid plan
credit rating score of a subscriber
device model and technology used
demographic breakdown by age and nationality
never_use_for: Subscription IDs, MSISDN Index, base line type (Prepaid/Postpaid), Activation Source, Sign-up method, VAS/bundles, lifecycle status (Active/Grace), loan amounts

description: This is the consolidated usage and revenue fact table used for segmentation, ARPU tracking, and overall subscriber spending. It provides the most granular breakdown of usage and aggregated revenue: data usage and revenue, voice usage split by on-net (same network) and off-net, IDD, SMS usage, and roaming. Critically, this table tracks overall 'Prepaid Subscription Revenue', 'Prepay Spending', and prepaid revenue breakdown by service type. Use this table when the query needs detailed usage breakdowns (like on-net vs off-net from bundles), monthly/rolling trend comparisons, ARPU calculations, GCC roaming data, or total prepaid subscription revenue/spending.
granularity: one record per subscriber per snapshot date
example_queries: total prepaid subscription revenue over a rolling 5-week period
prepaid subscription spending in the last week
on-net vs off-net voice usage from local bundle offers
ARPU trend and prepaid segment profitability
prepaid revenue breakdown by service type
never_use_for: individual product activation events, campaign tracking, audience segments, recharge channels, plan changes, real-time events, profile informations

description: This table captures near real-time transactional events AND dynamic billing/lifecycle statuses. It stores voice/SMS/data session events, recharge transactions, and digital wallet transactions (including Partner/Merchant IDs). Crucially, it tracks all loan and credit activity (airtime advances, device loans, outstanding debt, loan recovery status) and dynamic lifecycle status changes (Active, Grace, suspended, inactive). Use this table for real-time events, OR anytime a query asks for loan balances, debt, loan recovery status, or current lifecycle/subscription state (Active/Grace).
granularity: one record per subscriber per event or state change
example_queries: what is the customer's current lifecycle status (Active or Grace)
subscriber's remaining loan amount or outstanding debt
current Loan Recovery Status
payment Merchant identifier or Partner ID
real-time voice call happening now
never_use_for: demographics, base plan migrations (use Profile), summarized 30-day/90-day aggregated usage metrics, campaign tracking
